topTargets.name: Omar Cooper Jr.
topTargets.reasoning: Best blended value at 1.12 across all three boards: Consensus #12 Tier 2 (NYJ landing spot), RSP #15 DoT 83.9 with Deebo Samuel clone upside, MFL ADP #27. With WR:4 the thinnest skill room and 50/25/25 weighting, Cooper is the only player where all three boards point to this slot.

topTargets.name: Chris Brazzell II
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #15 Tier 2 (CAR), RSP #12 DoT 84.2 with George Pickens comp — RSP slightly higher than Consensus, which a medium-affinity GM rewards. ADP #48 is a discount. Direct fit for thin WR room and a ceiling play with 10 contracts expiring (need youth that pays in 2027+).

topTargets.name: Emmett Johnson
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #13 Tier 2 (KC backfield) and RSP #8 DoT 84.6 — RSP grades him meaningfully higher than market (not in ADP top 30), which my 25% RSP slice rewards. KC opportunity is real even with RB:5 already, since 10 deals expire and dynasty value matters.

topTargets.name: Antonio Williams
topTargets.reasoning: Round 2 target at 2.12. Consensus #24 Tier 4 (WAS) but RSP #18 DoT 83.9 with Jayden Reed comp — exactly the RSP-favored Tier-4 Consensus player a medium-affinity GM grabs at pick ~28. ADP #55 confirms market discount. Stacks WR room cheaply.

topTargets.name: Bryce Lance
topTargets.reasoning: Round 3 target at 3.12. Consensus #38 Free Agents (NO) but RSP #22 DoT 83.5 with A.J. Green high-end comp. Late-round RSP lean fits medium-affinity bias when the cost is a 3rd-rounder with ~$500K base (taxi-able at $250K).

capPosture: Tight at $42.6M of $45M used ($2.4M space) — 1.12 rookie must go taxi (~$600K hit at 50%); a 22nd active body cut is likely needed before draft day with 10 contracts expiring.

wildcard.name: Skyler Bell
wildcard.reasoning: Pure RSP-love play: RSP #6 DoT 85.9 with Ja'Marr Chase high-end comp, but Consensus dropped him to #33 Free Agents and he's not in MFL ADP top 30. A medium-affinity GM with a 3rd-round flier and a thin WR room takes the RSP-only swing at 3.12 if Lance is gone.

summary: Vitside Mafia is a soft-rebuilder: 21 actives, TE:5 bloat, WR:4 thin, $2.4M cap, 10 expiring deals. The plan is WR-WR with the first two picks — Cooper Jr. at 1.12 because all three boards align, Antonio Williams or Brazzell at 2.12 as an RSP-tilted Consensus Tier-4 grab. Round 3 stays on the WR vine with an RSP darling (Lance, or Skyler Bell as the wildcard) since this GM's 25% RSP slice rewards Waldman calls the market hasn't priced. TE is off the board despite Stowers/Klare value — already 5 deep there.
